name: V:tM Cross-Splat — Garou & Glass Walkers
description: Cross-splat lorebook for V:tM chronicles engaging Garou (Werewolf) NPCs as diplomatic counterparts. Provides Garou cosmology, social structure, Glass Walker tribal specifics, and Cress Truce-relevant detail. Designed to layer with vtm_modern_nights.json and vtm_melbourne_by_night.json (or other Australian city supplements). Where W:tA canon has been adapted, notes are included in entries.

0.content: GAROU — what Kindred call lupines, what mortals call werewolves, what they call themselves: Garou, the children of Gaia, the warriors of the Triat. Garou are shapeshifters — born to mortal parents (one Garou, one usually a kinfolk human or kinfolk wolf), they undergo their First Change at puberty or somewhat later, gaining the ability to shift between five physical forms (full human, near-human glabro, war-form crinos, dire-wolf hispo, full wolf lupus) and access to Gifts taught by spirits. Garou cosmology centres on three primal forces: the WYLD (creation, chaos, untamed nature), the WEAVER (order, stasis, civilisation, technology, the structures that hold things), and the WYRM (originally balance and destruction-as-renewal, but corrupted in the modern era into a force of pure rot and decay). Garou were created by Gaia — the Earth-spirit, mother to all — to defend Her against Wyrm corruption. They consider themselves at war. They consider this war ongoing. They consider the war to be losing. Garou call the current period the Apocalypse — a slow-rolling end times in which the corrupted Wyrm spreads through Pentex and similar mortal-corporate infrastructure, the Weaver's calcification freezes the Wyld out of urban environments, and Garou casualties accumulate without replacement. From the Kindred perspective: Garou are dangerous, rare (perhaps 10,000 globally), spirit-touched, do not negotiate with corruption, and consider Kindred existence at minimum unnatural and at maximum a Wyrm-corruption symptom. Cross-supernatural diplomacy with Garou is therefore difficult, expensive, and largely conducted by the rare tribe that has cultivated diplomatic competence: the Glass Walkers.

1.content: GLASS WALKERS — the Garou tribe of urban specialists, formed historically through the merger of the older Iron Riders (Roman-era urban Garou) and other city-adapted lineages, formalised as the Glass Walkers in the early 20th century with technological-modernity emphasis. Population estimated 1,200 globally, of whom approximately 60-80 are in Australia across all septs. Glass Walkers are considered 'least Garou' by traditionalist tribes (Get of Fenris, Red Talons, Wendigo, Uktena and others) because they have adapted to the Weaver's world — cities, technology, modern institutions, mortal-administrative structures — in ways those tribes consider themselves religiously prohibited from doing. The Glass Walkers' counter-argument: someone has to fight the Wyrm where the Wyrm actually lives, which in 2025 means in cities, in corporations, in financial systems, in computer networks. Glass Walkers operate from urban septs (the Royal Park sept in Melbourne is one), maintain extensive kinfolk networks in police, academic, business, and government institutions, work fluently with mortal modernity, and carry the unique tribal capability of cross-supernatural diplomatic standing. Most Garou tribes consider negotiation with Kindred categorically impossible. Glass Walkers consider it difficult, costly, occasionally necessary, and structurally feasible if the Kindred counterpart is competent and the diplomatic infrastructure is built carefully. The Cress Truce is the world's most successful Glass-Walker-led cross-supernatural diplomatic accord, and it is studied by other Glass Walker septs globally as a model. From the Kindred diplomatic perspective: if you must negotiate with Garou, find a Glass Walker; if you cannot find a Glass Walker, do not negotiate.

2.content: GAROU SOCIAL STRUCTURE — three nested levels: pack, sept, tribe. PACK is the smallest unit, typically 3-7 Garou bonded through a totem-spirit-mediated ritual; pack-bonds are genuinely supernatural (the totem spirit witnesses and reinforces the bond) rather than purely political. Multiple packs may share a sept. SEPT is the multi-pack social-religious organisation centered on a CAERN — a sacred place where the Gauntlet (the barrier between physical world and Umbra, the spirit world) is thin and where the sept conducts collective rituals. A sept's membership includes all packs working that caern plus unaffiliated solo Garou with caern-access rights plus the sept's KINFOLK (mortal humans related to Garou members by blood or marriage who carry Garou heritage without First Change). The Royal Park sept in Melbourne includes 30 Garou plus 50+ kinfolk; this is typical mid-size. SEPT FORMAL HIERARCHY includes ranked positions: SEPTLEADER (overall authority, requires Athro or Elder rank), WYRD WARDER (combat and threat-response, Adren+ rank), MASTER OF THE RITE (senior ritualist, typically Elder rank), TRUTHCATCHER (judicial role for sept-internal disputes, typically Philodox auspice), MASTER OF THE CHALLENGE (rank-Challenge moderator), TALESINGER (sept historian, typically Galliard auspice), GATEKEEPER (boundary monitor between physical and Umbra, typically Theurge auspice), HEART-OF-THE-SEPT (senior kinfolk role, the medical/institutional/spiritual-continuity asset). Not all positions are filled in all septs; smaller septs combine roles. TRIBE is the larger affiliation that crosses multiple septs globally — Glass Walkers worldwide, Get of Fenris worldwide, etc. Tribal politics happen through Continental Sept Councils (regional) and Grand Moots (rare, global). Kindred encountering Garou should know which sept-rank and tribal-affiliation they are addressing; protocol differs significantly between e.g. a Cliath-rank Glass Walker scout and an Athro-rank Septleader.

3.content: GAROU RANKS — five formal ranks earned through accumulated RENOWN (the social currency of Garou society, awarded by witnesses for deeds and judged in three categories: GLORY for combat valour and bold action, HONOR for keeping oaths and following tradition, WISDOM for prudent judgement and successful diplomacy). Ranks in ascending order: CLIATH (newly Changed Garou, equivalent to apprentice; 0-2 years post-First-Change typical); FOSTERN (proven Garou, mid-junior; 2-8 years typical); ADREN (seasoned operational Garou, can lead packs; 8-20 years typical); ATHRO (mature Garou, can lead septs as Septleader; 20-50 years typical); ELDER (senior Garou, often serve as Masters of the Rite, sept advisors, or Continental Sept Council members; 50+ years typical, retirement-honor pathway begins around 70-75 years of age). Beyond the five standard ranks is the rare LEGENDARY rank, awarded by Grand Moot acknowledgment for deeds of historical significance — perhaps a dozen Garou globally hold legendary rank at any time. Rank ascension requires CHALLENGES — formal tests where the Garou demonstrates the renown they have accumulated through specific Challenge-rituals administered by the Master of the Challenge. Challenges typically include Glory-tests (combat or daring action), Honor-tests (oath-keeping demonstration), and Wisdom-tests (judgement scenarios or successful diplomatic outcomes). KINFOLK do not hold formal Garou ranks but are acknowledged in sept hierarchy through INSTITUTIONAL ROLES (Heart-of-the-Sept, Researcher, Liaison) that carry operational weight despite kinfolk-status structural limitations in Garou-internal hierarchy. Cross-supernatural diplomacy register: address Cliath-rank Garou as equals (peer-courtesy), address Fostern through Athro as senior-respected (formal courtesy), address Elder rank as elder-respected (deferential courtesy plus rank-acknowledgment phrasing). Kindred who fail rank-protocol with Garou produce minor diplomatic friction; consistent rank-protocol failure produces sept-level diplomatic refusal.

4.content: GAROU AUSPICES — every Garou is born under one of five moon phases at the moment of their birth, and the moon phase determines their AUSPICE (their lifelong social-religious role within Garou society). Auspice is innate and unchangeable. The five auspices: RAGABASH (born under new moon, the trickster-scout role; specialises in stealth, infiltration, asking the questions others cannot ask, breaking unwise traditions; the auspice most likely to engage in cross-supernatural diplomacy of an unconventional kind); THEURGE (crescent moon, the mystic-spirit-walker role; specialises in spirit-mediation, ritual practice, Umbra navigation, Gauntlet-walking; produces most Masters of the Rite and Gatekeepers); PHILODOX (half moon, the judge-mediator role; specialises in dispute resolution, formal oath-witnessing, sept-internal judicial work; produces most Truthcatchers and most cross-supernatural diplomats — Eliza Hawkins-Greene of Melbourne is Philodox); GALLIARD (gibbous moon, the historian-storyteller-musician role; specialises in sept memory, tribal history, ritual song, public morale; produces most Talesingers); AHROUN (full moon, the warrior role; specialises in combat, threat-response, defensive operations; produces most Wyrd Warders and combat-specialist pack leaders — Marcus Tan of the Royal Park sept is Ahroun). Auspice shapes the Garou's specialised Gifts (auspice-specific Gifts are taught only to that auspice's holders), their conventional sept role, and their personal-temperament register. Kindred encountering Garou should attempt to identify auspice early in interaction — a Ragabash will engage differently from a Philodox, who will engage differently from an Ahroun. Cross-supernatural diplomacy is structurally easier with Philodox counterparts (whose auspice is built for it) and structurally harder with Ahroun counterparts (whose auspice is built for combat against threats). The auspice distinction is genuinely supernatural — born under the moon-phase, the Garou cannot change auspice and would not consider it sensible to want to.

5.content: GAROU PHYSICAL FORMS — every Garou can shift between five forms: HOMID (full human, the form most Garou use for mortal-society interaction; Glass Walkers spend most of their time in homid); GLABRO (near-human, taller and more muscular, faintly bestial features, useful for intimidation in mortal contexts where full crinos would be Masquerade-violating); CRINOS (war form — half-human-half-wolf hybrid, approximately three metres tall, fully bipedal, claws, fangs, the form Garou use for combat and for sacred ritual; categorically Masquerade-violating to mortal observers; produces an instinctive supernatural-terror response in unprepared mortals called the DELIRIUM that erases or distorts their memory of the encounter); HISPO (dire-wolf form, approximately the size of a large bear, four-legged, used for tracking and combat in non-urban environments); LUPUS (full wolf form, used for stealth in non-urban environments and for ritual purposes). Form-shifting requires expenditure of RAGE (the Garou's spiritual-emotional fuel; see Rage entry). Crinos is the form Garou are most often imagined in by mortal folklore — and it is the form in which they are most physically dangerous. A crinos-form Garou can match an 8th-generation methuselah-track Kindred in close combat (this is the calibration: not invincible against true elders, but genuinely competitive). Cross-supernatural diplomacy is conducted in homid form as a matter of strict protocol — appearing in glabro signals threat, appearing in crinos signals war. The Cress Truce explicitly requires homid-form-only attendance at all formal Truce Council meetings, and violation of this protocol is treated as Truce breach. Eliza Hawkins-Greene has spent her entire Septleadership in homid form during cross-supernatural diplomatic contexts and has not made an exception even in moments of significant emotional pressure.

6.content: RAGE — the Garou spiritual-emotional fuel, parallel-but-distinct from Kindred Hunger, that powers form-shifting, Gift use, and combat capability. Rage is generated through rest, ritual, contact with Wyld-aligned spirits and places, and (controversially) through direct combat with Wyrm-corruption. Rage is spent through form-shifting, Gift use, and certain combat manoeuvres. Rage is also the source of FRENZY for Garou — analogous to Kindred Beast frenzy but mechanically different. Garou frenzy types: FOX FRENZY (terror flight, parallel to Kindred Terror Frenzy), THRALL OF THE WYRM (Wyrm-corruption-induced loss of control, no Kindred parallel), and BERSERK FRENZY (combat-rage loss of control, parallel to Kindred Fury Frenzy and the most diplomatically dangerous Garou state). A Garou in Berserk frenzy will attack any non-pack member within reach, will not discriminate Kindred-vs-mortal-vs-Wyrm-corruption, and is functionally uncontrollable until the frenzy resolves (typically minutes to hours). Cross-supernatural diplomacy is conducted under the unspoken rule that NO PARTY PROVOKES GAROU FRENZY. Kindred who use Presence-3 or higher on Garou diplomatic counterparts risk triggering frenzy through emotional interference; Kindred who deploy Dominate on Garou diplomatic counterparts risk triggering frenzy through perceived spiritual violation; Kindred whose Hunger surges visibly during diplomatic meeting risk triggering frenzy through Garou perception of predatory intent. Glass Walker septs train extensively on frenzy management; senior Glass Walkers (Athro and Elder rank) can suppress incipient frenzy through ritual breath-techniques. Eliza Hawkins-Greene has not entered frenzy during seven years of Septleadership; this is itself part of her diplomatic credibility.

7.content: THE UMBRA — the spirit world, the parallel reflection of physical reality where spirits dwell and Garou conduct much of their religious practice. The Umbra is separated from the physical world by the GAUNTLET, a barrier that is thicker in urban-Weaver environments and thinner at caerns and wild places. Garou (particularly Theurges) can step sideways across the Gauntlet through ritual or Gift use and operate in the Umbra. Most spirits cannot easily cross the Gauntlet in the other direction without significant effort or thinning conditions. SPIRITS — entities native to the Umbra, of varying types: NATURE SPIRITS (representing physical-world phenomena — bird-spirits, river-spirits, weather-spirits), ELEMENTAL SPIRITS (representing primal forces), TOTEM SPIRITS (greater spirits that bond with Garou packs and septs as patrons), ANCESTOR SPIRITS (deceased Garou whose essence remains in the Umbra and can be consulted by their descendants and successors through ritual), BANES (Wyrm-corrupted spirits, hostile to Garou, often bound to mortal corruption-vectors), WEAVER-SPIRITS (technology-spirits and order-spirits, neutral-to-friendly to Glass Walkers specifically). The Royal Park sept's totem is COCKATOO (an urban-bushland liminal totem appropriate to a Glass Walker sept on Wurundjeri country); the totem-Cockatoo's spiritual presence at the caern is the binding-witness for the sept's pack-bonds. Don Abacus reached Final Death in 2003 and his ANCESTOR SPIRIT can be consulted at the Royal Park caern through ritual conducted by the Master of the Rite (Diana Cole) — the consultation is rare, costly in spiritual resources, and treated with extreme respect. From the Kindred perspective: most Kindred cannot perceive the Umbra (Auspex 5 with specific applications can do so partially); most Kindred cannot perceive spirits (same constraint); the Hecata's Oblivion-Necromancy works on a different metaphysical plane (the realm of the dead, not the Garou Umbra of living-world spirits) but produces overlap effects that the Hecata and the Glass Walkers have been studying together since 1924.

8.content: GAROU GIFTS — the Garou equivalent of Kindred Disciplines, taught to individual Garou by spirits in exchange for service or knowledge, organised by tribe-specific Gifts (Glass Walkers have technology-affinity Gifts, urban-environment Gifts, social-mortal Gifts), auspice-specific Gifts (Ahroun combat Gifts, Theurge spirit Gifts, Philodox judgement Gifts), and breed-specific Gifts (Homid-breed lineage Gifts, Lupus-breed lineage Gifts, Metis-breed lineage Gifts — Metis being Garou born of two Garou parents, considered cursed in most tribes including Glass Walkers). Common Glass Walker Gifts include CONTROL SIMPLE MACHINE (manipulate technology with thought), JAM TECHNOLOGY (disable nearby technology), BRIDGE WALKER (transit Gauntlet through urban-infrastructure paths), TRICK SHOT (firearms accuracy enhancement), and CYBERSENSES (digital-information perception). Common Glass Walker Philodox Gifts (Eliza's specialty range) include TRUTH OF GAIA (detect spoken lies), STRENGTH OF PURPOSE (resist mental-emotional interference including Kindred Presence/Dominate at lower levels), and SCENT OF THE TRUE FORM (perceive supernatural creature actual nature regardless of cover). RITES — Garou ritual practices, conducted by Theurge specialists at caerns or sept-significant locations, organised into formal categories (Mystic Rites, Caern Rites, Punishment Rites, Death Rites, Renown Rites, Seasonal Rites). The 1924 Cress Truce was bound by RITE OF BINDING — a specific Theurge ritual that spirit-witnesses cross-faction agreements at sept-acceptable level; the Truce's century-spanning persistence is partly attributable to the spirit-binding's continued recognition in Umbra. The 2024 centennial renewal required re-binding through the same Rite, which Diana Cole (Royal Park sept Master of the Rite) and Sister Caterina Calabria-Donato (Calabria Branch family priest) co-conducted under unprecedented cross-supernatural ritual cooperation.

9.content: KINFOLK — mortal humans related to Garou by blood or marriage who carry Garou heritage without undergoing First Change. Kinfolk are critical to Garou society in three ways: as PARENTS (Garou children are born to one Garou parent and one kinfolk parent in the typical pattern; pure-Garou children, Metis, are considered cursed in most tribes), as INFRASTRUCTURE (sept operations require mortal-society institutional access — police, academic, medical, legal, business — that Garou themselves cannot maintain in homid form continuously), and as INHERITED CARRIERS (kinfolk's children may undergo First Change in subsequent generations, maintaining the tribe's population). Kinfolk carry several supernatural traits: KINFOLK AWARENESS (can perceive Garou presence at conversational distance and detect basic supernatural-creature presence at closer range, an ambient sensitivity rather than active Auspex-equivalent detection), DELIRIUM IMMUNITY (kinfolk do not suffer the supernatural-terror Delirium response that ordinary mortals experience when seeing crinos-form Garou), REFLEXIVE RESISTANCE (kinfolk have meaningful but partial resistance to Disciplines like Dominate and Presence at the lower levels), and ENHANCED HEALING BASELINE (kinfolk heal somewhat faster than ordinary mortals from physical injury). Kinfolk are CONSIDERED FULL SEPT MEMBERS in operational terms despite formal kinfolk-status structural limitation in Garou-internal hierarchy. Glass Walker septs in particular treat kinfolk as core operational units, not auxiliary mortal-network. The Royal Park sept's three kinfolk operational members (Sarah Brennan, James Patterson, Helen Marquez) are full members of the operational unit; their kinfolk-status is structural rather than peripheral. From the Kindred perspective: kinfolk are not appropriate feeding targets — feeding from kinfolk is a Cress Truce violation — and Kindred who do not respect this distinction will discover that kinfolk awareness produces near-perfect identification of Kindred presence, which the kinfolk will report to their sept within minutes.

10.content: THE WYRM — originally one of the three Triat forces alongside Wyld and Weaver, charged with destruction-as-renewal (the necessary breaking-down that allows new growth). Corrupted in pre-recorded antiquity through events Garou cosmology describes as the Wyrm's binding by the Weaver, which drove the Wyrm mad and transformed it from balance-force into pure-corruption-force. The Wyrm in 2025 is the source of all Garou-recognised supernatural corruption: PENTEX (the global mortal-corporate conglomerate operating as the Wyrm's primary modern manifestation, with subsidiary corporations across pollution, weapons, drugs, financial fraud, and cultural-corruption industries; recognised by Garou as primary mortal-world target); BLACK SPIRAL DANCERS (the corrupted thirteenth tribe of Garou who fell to Wyrm influence and now serve it actively; perhaps 600 globally; considered enemies-of-the-blood by all uncorrupted Garou); FOMORI (mortals possessed by Wyrm-spirits, varying in capability from mildly-corrupted bureaucrats to fully-transformed combat-monsters); BANES (Wyrm-corrupted spirits operating in the Umbra and producing fomori through possession). From the Kindred perspective: Garou perceive most Kindred as Wyrm-adjacent or Wyrm-corrupted because Kindred undeath and Vitae-feeding registers to Garou supernatural senses as life-cycle violation; this is the structural reason cross-supernatural diplomacy with Garou is difficult, because Garou are not casually willing to negotiate with what their cosmology categorises as corruption-symptom. Glass Walkers specifically have, across decades, developed the position that some Kindred lineages and individuals are NOT Wyrm-corrupted in the operational sense — that the Hecata's death-respect tradition produces Vitae-resonance distinguishable from corruption-resonance, that long-Humanity Kindred (7+) produce energetic patterns Glass Walker Theurges can read as legitimate, and that diplomatic relationships are structurally feasible with these Kindred even though most Garou will never accept this reasoning. The Cress Truce is the operational embodiment of this Glass Walker position. Bishop Callista Vox's Brisbane Sabbat reads to Glass Walker Theurges as Wyrm-adjacent in ways the Cress Truce specifically excludes from cooperative protection; this is why the Truce does not extend to Brisbane.

11.content: THE RAZOR WARS — the brutal continental Kindred-Garou conflict that consumed Australia from 1907 to 1924 and ended with the most successful inter-supernatural treaty of the modern era. See PART 2 of this supplement for full historical detail. SUMMARY: The conflict's origins were colonial — as European Kindred expanded their feeding territories across rapidly-urbanising Australia, they pushed into bushland and country regions claimed by Garou septs (Glass Walkers in cities and edge-bushland, Wendigo-equivalent Indigenous-affiliated tribes in remote country, Bone Gnawers in inner-city poverty zones, and others) under the Triat's mandate. The first significant incident occurred 14 February 1907 when a Brisbane Ventrue's hunting expedition into the Glasshouse Mountains north of Brisbane was destroyed by a Glass Walker pack led by the Garou named WALKER-OF-IRON-ROADS (Don Abacus's predecessor as Continental Sept Council senior member). Camarilla retaliation followed within weeks; Garou continental mobilisation followed within months. Over seventeen years, the Razor Wars killed approximately 400 Kindred (a catastrophic loss representing perhaps 30% of the continent's pre-war Kindred population) and an estimated 60-90 Garou (a catastrophic loss representing perhaps 25% of the continent's pre-war Garou population). Both populations were so reduced that the war exhausted itself. The conflict ended through the negotiation of the CRESS TRUCE in 1923-1924 by Luisa Calabria (Giovanni, now Hecata) for the Kindred side and DON ABACUS (Glass Walker urban-elder) for the Garou side. The Truce divided Australia into Kindred Zones, Garou Zones, and Neutral Zones with detailed protocols, and has held with minor revisions for 101 years. The Razor Wars are the foundational political event of modern Australian supernatural politics; every continental decision is partly downstream of them. Modern Australian Kindred and Garou treat the war as cautionary history rather than as victorious or defeated past — both populations recognise that catastrophic mutual destruction was the actual outcome.

12.content: THE CRESS TRUCE — negotiated 1923-1924 at the Cress Hotel on Bourke Street in Melbourne (the hotel was demolished in 1937 and the site is now an office building, but the Truce retains its name in memorial), formally signed 14 February 1924 (seventeen years to the day after the Glasshouse Mountains incident that began the Razor Wars). See PART 3 of this supplement for full protocol detail. SUMMARY: The Truce is approximately seventy pages of detailed cross-supernatural law negotiated by Luisa Calabria (Giovanni) and Don Abacus (Glass Walker) over six months of formal proceedings at the Cress Hotel, with delegations from approximately fourteen Kindred sects/clans/independent operators and from approximately eleven Garou tribes operating in Australia. The Truce establishes: ZONE DESIGNATION SYSTEM (Kindred Zones — urban cores of major cities; Garou Zones — bushland and parks plus specific urban districts; Neutral Zones — the remainder, governed by case-by-case protocols); INCIDENT RESPONSE PROTOCOLS (the 72-hour emergency response procedure; the quarterly Truce Council; the dispute resolution mechanism); MORTAL-PROTECTION COOPERATION (joint protocols for Masquerade-protection, kinfolk-protection, and protection of mortal communities affected by supernatural activity); CROSS-SUPERNATURAL THREAT COORDINATION (joint response protocols for Sabbat, Twilight Project, Pentex, and other shared threats); and SPIRIT-BINDING (the Theurge Rite of Binding that spirit-witnesses the Truce at Garou-recognised level). The Truce has been revised three times: minor 1962 revisions clarifying urban-zone boundary protocols, minor 1989 revisions adding Five-Eyes-cooperation-era Twilight Project response protocols, and the substantive 2024 centennial revisions reflecting eighty years of accumulated working-relationship and incorporating Hecata-line modernisation language post-1999 reformation. The 2024 centennial renewal was negotiated by Sofia Calabria-Hecata (granddaughter of Luisa) and Eliza Hawkins-Greene (Septleader, successor to the Don-Abacus-Marcus-Cobalt lineage). The Truce is studied globally as the model for cross-supernatural diplomatic infrastructure.

13.content: OTHER GAROU TRIBES IN AUSTRALIA — beyond Glass Walkers, several other tribes maintain operational presence on the continent. BONE GNAWERS (the urban-poor specialist tribe; population approximately 30 in Australia; primary presence in inner Melbourne, inner Sydney, Brisbane West End, Kings Cross; operate from informal urban sites rather than formal caerns; cooperative-friendly with Glass Walkers in Melbourne but with their own diplomatic priorities centred on mortal-poverty community defence; the Melbourne Bone Gnawer presence operates under the informal leadership of PADDY BOON, an Adren-rank Galliard who is the Bone Gnawers' senior continental representative). CHILDREN OF GAIA (the peacemaker tribe; population approximately 15 in Australia; primary presence in regional Victoria, Tasmania, and Northern Rivers NSW; specialise in cross-tribal and cross-supernatural diplomatic mediation; correct-and-warm relationship with Glass Walkers; the Children of Gaia were instrumental as third-party witnesses during the 1923-1924 Cress Truce negotiation and continue to provide diplomatic support during major Truce events). BLACK FURIES (the matriarchal warrior tribe; population approximately 12 in Australia; primary presence in regional NSW and Victoria; allied with Glass Walkers in the Australian context though with their own gendered-political priorities). GET OF FENRIS (the combat-traditionalist tribe; population approximately 8 in Australia, very small for the tribe globally; primary presence in regional WA and NT; relationship with Glass Walkers is correct-but-cool, the Get of Fenris consider the Cress Truce ideologically suspect though they have not violated it). UKTENA (the spirit-walker tribe with traditional North American Indigenous affiliation; population approximately 5 in Australia, transplanted from Pacific Northwest sept connections; small presence in Northern Rivers NSW; respectful-and-distant relationship with Glass Walkers). The CANONICAL WERE-CANID PRESENCE in Indigenous Australian context is canonically NOT modeled as standard Garou — the Glass Walker tribe and adjacent tribes have respectful working acknowledgment that Aboriginal Australian supernatural traditions have their own protocols distinct from the Garou Nation, and cross-tradition diplomatic cooperation in Australia operates through informal Indigenous-supernatural-elder consultation that the Royal Park sept has maintained for several decades. Tau Larrakia of the Footscray Crew bridges this distinction in his unusual position as Indigenous Garou-cooperation-trained Kindred. ADAPTATION NOTE: This kit's handling of Indigenous Australian supernatural context is intentionally light — it acknowledges the traditions exist and matter while not attempting to render them in colonial-RPG-cosmological terms; chronicles engaging this material more deeply should consult Indigenous-Australian sensitivity readers and source material directly.

14.content: CROSS-SPLAT DIPLOMATIC PROTOCOL — the practical guidance for Kindred-side characters engaging Garou diplomatic counterparts in scenes. The texture: cross-supernatural diplomacy with Glass Walkers in Australia operates as FORMAL INSTITUTIONAL NEGOTIATION rather than as warm-friendly cooperation. The two parties are treaty-counterparts, not allies. The Cress Truce holds because the people running it are competent and because the alternative (Razor Wars repetition) is recognised by both sides as catastrophic. Tonal expectations for scenes: GREETINGS are formal — rank-acknowledgment phrases, standard openings, no first-name familiarity from Kindred-side until specifically invited; PHYSICAL COURTESY includes maintaining homid-form by Garou, maintaining Auspex-baseline (not surge) by Kindred, no Discipline use during meetings without prior protocol agreement, no visible Hunger by Kindred; AGENDA STRUCTURE follows Cress Truce procedural conventions (formal greetings, statement of purpose, formal exchange of positions, deliberation, formal exchange of conclusions, formal closure); LANGUAGE register is formal modern Australian English with occasional Garou-traditional-formal phrases ('I speak as Septleader,' 'this party witnesses,' 'the Truce holds these proceedings'); KINFOLK PRESENCE at meetings is normal and kinfolk are full operational participants; SPIRIT WITNESS is acknowledged in formal proceedings (the meeting is, to Garou senses, occurring within range of spirit-observers and the parties speak with that awareness). For Kindred-side characters new to cross-splat diplomacy: avoid casual touch, avoid sudden movements, avoid feeding-references in conversation, avoid Sabbat-references except in operational-coordination contexts, do not interrupt Garou rank-elders, address Septleaders by title rather than name, accept tea or water when offered (mortal-Australian hospitality protocols apply alongside cross-supernatural protocols), do not bring uninvited Kindred companions, and respect kinfolk operational voice. Cross-supernatural diplomatic competence is itself a Status-equivalent within Camarilla (Sister Fatima Salih-El's Sheriff-cum-diplomat role gains her continental recognition) and within Anarch contexts (Tau Larrakia's Indigenous Garou-cooperation reflexes are part of his Footscray Crew operational value). Kindred who develop genuine cross-supernatural diplomatic competence are rare and valuable; this is a specialty that pays compound returns across decades.
